note: Enhanced PR Freelance pipeline scrape. 7-day search window. Web-search sourced (no LinkedIn bot scraping). This run is funding-news heavy: the strongest signals were recent venture raises in finance/InsurTech/B2B-SaaS where a scaling narrative + thought-leadership surface opens up, plus one agency-network expansion (H/Advisors) as an overflow/white-label play. Deduplicated against pr-freelance scrapes 2026-04-30 through 2026-05-14 and daily scrapes. Reserv (already captured 2026-05-12) and Infinite Global / Dukas Linden (already captured 2026-05-08) were excluded as duplicates. Astrada ($3.8M seed, autonomous finance) was scored and excluded at combined 52 (below the 55 threshold) — seed-stage, likely no comms budget yet; worth re-checking after a Series A. All 5 leads below score 55+.

leads.name: H/Advisors
leads.industry: Global Strategic & Financial Communications Agency Network
leads.location: Global network (Paris HQ); actively expanding US footprint
leads.compensationText: Not stated — agency overflow / white-label engagements typically project + hourly + retainer
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — senior overflow / white-label execution capacity as the network builds out its US presence
leads.notes: TOP LEAD this run (combined 72, B). H/Advisors is a global strategic and financial communications agency network; executive chairman Stephane Fouks has publicly named the US among the group's 2026 expansion targets (acquisitions + new office openings). A comms agency network building out a new territory is a textbook capacity-expansion moment — they staff new-market work with senior contractors before headcount catches up. Buyer type (agency) and sector (financial communications) are both dead-center in Mark's ICP. ACTION: Direct outreach to H/Advisors' US-build leadership positioning senior US-based white-label / overflow execution capacity — financial and corporate narrative, media relations, executive comms — emphasize plug-in-without-ramp as they stand up US client work. Relationship play, not an application.

leads.name: Corgi
leads.industry: AI-Native Full-Stack Insurance Carrier (InsurTech) — startup-focused, expanding into trucking
leads.fundingStage: Series B (May 2026, $160M led by TCV at $1.3B valuation; >$268M total raised)
leads.compensationText: Not stated — cold approach; fractional / project / retainer typical for post-Series-B scaling-narrative work
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — fractional / project comms support for the Series B scaling narrative and trucking-vertical launch
leads.notes: Combined 66, B grade. Corgi is an AI-native, full-stack insurance carrier built for startups (founders Emily Yuan and Nico Laqua); it raised a $160M Series B led by TCV at a $1.3B valuation, announced 2026-05-06 — one of the fastest Series-A-to-unicorn runs in recent InsurTech history, valuation doubled from $630M in four months, >$268M total raised. The company is moving into new verticals, starting with trucking. Two comms surfaces open up at once: a Series B scaling/leadership narrative and a net-new vertical launch. Sector is InsurTech — adjacent to, not dead-center in, Mark's finance/PE ICP, so fit is capped at 40; intent is solid on the back of the fresh raise and explicit expansion. ACTION: Cold outreach to Corgi's comms/marketing lead (or directly to Emily Yuan) positioning senior fractional/project support — Series B narrative, trucking-vertical launch comms, founder thought leadership, carrier-credibility storytelling. Move while the raise is still fresh.

leads.name: XBOW
leads.industry: Autonomous Offensive Security / AI Cybersecurity (B2B SaaS)
leads.location: United States (Seattle mailbox / distributed); international expansion underway
leads.fundingStage: Series C extension (May 2026, +$35M; $155M total Series C; >$270M overall; >$1B valuation)
leads.compensationText: Not stated — cold approach; fractional / project / retainer typical for funding-driven narrative work
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — fractional / project comms support for the Series C narrative and international GTM expansion
leads.notes: Combined 63, B grade. XBOW is an autonomous offensive-security company (AI-driven, expert-level security testing at machine speed), founded by Oege de Moor — the engineer who created GitHub Copilot. It secured a $35M Series C extension announced 2026-05-06 (total Series C now $155M, >$270M overall, >$1B valuation), with several customers turning investors. 100+ customers including Moderna and Seznam; passed 250 employees and scaling GTM, engineering and operations; funding earmarked for international growth. B2B SaaS is an explicit fit sector in Mark's ICP. Comms surface: Series C narrative, a marquee founder story, customer-credibility case studies, and an international-expansion announcement calendar. ACTION: Cold outreach to XBOW's comms/marketing lead positioning senior fractional/project support — funding narrative, founder thought leadership, category-defining 'autonomous offensive security' positioning, international launch comms. Note: cybersecurity, not finance — Mark's angle here is execution range, not sector expertise.

leads.name: Fun
leads.location: United States; expanding to APAC (new Singapore office)
leads.fundingStage: Series A (announced May 2026, $72M co-led by Multicoin Capital & SignalFire)
leads.compensationText: Not stated — cold approach; fractional / project / retainer typical for Series-A scaling comms
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — fractional / project comms support for the Series A narrative, APAC expansion and hiring ramp
leads.notes: Combined 58, C grade. Fun is a global payments-infrastructure company that powers deposits, withdrawals and settlement flows for leading crypto apps (Polymarket, Lighter, Aave) and processes over $18B/year. It raised a $72M Series A co-led by Multicoin Capital and SignalFire, announced ~2026-05-01, and is putting the money toward staffing (team of ~30), an APAC expansion (new Singapore office) and potential acquisitions. Payments/fintech is core to Mark's ICP; the catch is a ~30-person team that may not yet have a formal comms function — which is exactly the 'founder/marketing team needs external help' profile. ACTION: Cold outreach to Fun's founder or marketing lead positioning fractional/project comms — Series A narrative, payments-infrastructure category storytelling, APAC-launch comms. Lower priority than H/Advisors and Corgi but a clean fintech fit; treat as a keep-warm with one good touch.

leads.name: Sahi
leads.industry: AI-Powered Stock Trading / Brokerage Platform (Fintech / Capital Markets)
leads.fundingStage: Series B (April 2026, $33M led by Accel at $200M valuation)
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — fractional / project comms support for the Series B narrative and product-line expansion
leads.notes: Combined 56, C grade — LOWEST priority this run, included for optionality only. Sahi is a Bengaluru-based AI-powered stock-trading / brokerage platform (founder Dale Vaz, former Swiggy CTO) that raised a $33M Series B led by Accel at a $200M valuation, announced 2026-04-30. Strong sector fit on paper — capital markets / brokerage fintech, multiple new product lines (MTF, commodities, mutual funds), 24x trade-volume growth. Two real drawbacks: (1) GEOGRAPHY — Sahi is India-based and India-market-focused, a mismatch for a US freelance PR operator; any engagement would be remote and likely India-media-centric, outside Mark's network. (2) RECENCY — the raise is ~15 days old, outside the tight window. ACTION: Only worth a touch if Mark wants international/remote exposure or has India media contacts; otherwise skip. Do not invest beyond one exploratory note.
